code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test18-NOV-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria and glucosuria, with low specific gravity, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction. No evidence of urinary tract infection is present. The findings are consistent with renal impairment, possibly diabetic nephropathy or another chronic kidney disease. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
